claims.claim: We investigate whether an adaptive evidence packing mechanism, when integrated into an end-to-end retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) inference pipeline, can simultaneously improve answer quality, citation accuracy, token efficiency, and latency under fixed token budgets.

evidence_refs.quote: # Adaptive Evidence Packing for Budget-Constrained RAG: An End-to-End Integration Study

claims.notes: Historical backfill linked the claim to already published artifacts; this is not independent source revalidation.

claims.claim: Using SQuAD v1.1 dev data with a local DistilBERT extractive reader and a sparse paragraph retriever, we compare three packing strategies—raw budget truncation, pure evidence-span carpentry, and an adaptive hybrid gate—across 120-, 160-, and 220-token budgets on 100 answerable examples per condition.

evidence_refs.quote: # Adaptive Evidence Packing for Budget-Constrained RAG: An End-to-End Integration Study

claims.notes: Historical backfill linked the claim to already published artifacts; this is not independent source revalidation.

claims.claim: The adaptive hybrid improves exact-match F1 over raw truncation by +2.98, +2.38, and +1.40 points at budgets 120, 160, and 220 respectively, while also improving citation/source hit rates and reducing both token consumption and total latency.
